import io
import string
import list

fn parse_tokens(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut start = i
    mut scanning = true
    while scanning and start < n
      match string.byte_at(s, start)
      when Some(c)
        if c == 32 or c == 9
          start = start + 1
        else
          scanning = false
        end
      when None
        scanning = false
      end
    end
    if start >= n
      i = n
    else
      mut stop = start
      mut finding = true
      while finding and stop < n
        match string.byte_at(s, stop)
        when Some(c)
          if c == 32 or c == 9
            finding = false
          else
            stop = stop + 1
          end
        when None
          finding = false
        end
      end
      let token = string.substring(s, start, stop)
      match string.parse_int(token)
      when Some(v)
        result = list.push(result, v)
      when None
        ()
      end
      i = stop
    end
  end
  result
end

fn find_index(_ xs: List<Int>, _ target: Int) -> Option<Int>
  list.index_of(xs, target)
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_tokens(line1)

let target = match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
when Some(v)
  v
when None
  0
end

match find_index(nums, target)
when Some(idx)
  println("index=#{idx}")
when None
  println("not_found")
end
